Her other character arcs were her mysterious backstory, her relationship with dexter, and being a reliable companion
all of which ended after arc 1
so she had a midlife crisis at the age of 21
I do think she needs a hobby
losing the keeper hit her way too hard
And I wish we'd get to see that, honestly!

Her relationship with Dexter was only ever really a side bit until the finale, where we never really got to see the emotional fallout, and we already know her mysterious backstory (also without the emotional fallout for her). I really hope we get to see more of how Ramona herself is handling things in that regard

Also, I actually kind of think that one thing that would already help is having her and William specifically interact more to stress the differences between them. I get the impression that those 2 and the MC are supposed to form a classic id-ego-superego trio, but because both of them only really interact with the MC, we basically end up with two really similar basic dynamics of "stern logic-focused person and their emotional mess of a friend"
Ramona just needs a psyche deepdive
Except one of them has a bunch of inner conflicts and emotional friction going on and the other is basically a case of "what you see is what you get"
she was too chained to her work for all of the story so we never get to see her doing other things or other parts of her personality
Yeah, a proper one that's focused on her as an individual

"my hobbies are going on missions" girl

Her personality is
- loves the MC
- loyal
- stern
- rational
- mission-focused

but you spend most of arc 1 rescuing him
Oh most of what I'm talking about comes in in Arc 2
It's great, it really improves him as a character, but it unfortunately really highlights how Ramona has not gotten that same treatment (yet, hopefully)
my impression of kadath-william is that he was similarly reliable but less straight laced, willing to bend the rules and throw himself into danger
he also just filled a different narrative role for the most part
the face of "mythag field support" rather than just a nameless other investigator

I mean, bending the rules, not really. He's extremely straight-laced. Throwing himself into danger, yes, that's part of the extra stuff he has going on, but it's not really recklessness so much as his own rational judgement
I don't want to spoil Arc 2 for you, but believe me, the comparison between him and Ramona is warranted, haha

演绎法

In english the name of her ult is "Mundus Decree"

In chinese it is 世界演绎法 which i would translate literally as "Deductive Law of the World" or "Universal Deductive Method"

So her exalt voiceline is actually just literally stating the name of her ult <:wisdom:1400771656510734356>
with this deductive method... i will uncover the universe!

Keyflare would be Silver Key Energy if they translated it word for word
It’s normal for us Bilinguals to notice differences in translation because there’s no perfect translation
Another interesting thing about Kathi translation
I think her WoD banner name? Or WoD name? Was called From Fomalhaut in English, which is a clear reference to Cthugha’s summoning chant;
But it’s actually called 死亡如约 (death as promised) in Chinese
because apparently, the Chinese translators of the original Lovecraftian novel added the phrase to Cthugha’s chant
When the original English chant DID NOT HAVE IT
So to preserve that Cthugha summon chant reference, they had to essentially rewrite the English
So all players would still get “ah this title references that chant” despite the phrasing being entirely different
her hidden texts in her banner UI is still the Cthugha chant but instead of Cthugha it’s her name in the chant
what en egoist
(jk)
It’s so funny because i actually tried to look into why the banner name was so different
And when i found out it’s all caused by some random Chinese novel translator deciding to add a cool sounding phrase into the novel, out of nowhere
Like apparently they decided to “translate” the bunch of nonsense th’ph’gh stuff into “death as promised”
And now the entire china thinks thats what it actually says,
wasnt the entire point of the chants that they were supposed to be incomprehensible
Like
Ph'nglui mglw'nafh Cthugha Fomalhaut n'gha-ghaa naf'l thagn! Iä! Cthugha!"
They “translated” this into
Though gone, not forgotten, awaiting at Fomalhaut, Cthugha pledges to you the vow of death! Descend now, Cthugha!
虽已逝去但并未遗忘，候于北落师门的克图格亚，向汝等许下死亡之诺！降临吧！克图格亚！
And i found out most of the “meaning” here is invented
lol
Including the pledges the vow of death part, the person who translated that novel into Chinese invented it, the author didn’t intend it
thats the literal worst translation ive ever seen
And Morimens writer, unaware, used the vow of death part for Kath’s WoD banner name
translation more like fanfiction
The reference to Cthugha would be obvious to all Chinese players because they all read the same translation
But English localizer, apparently, had to make it “From Fomalhaut” instead, because if they translated it loyally back, the reference is lost
Like the only readable parts in that chant are Cthugha and Fomalhaut, the rest was supposed to be gibberish…
holy shit cn exclusive fanfiction
poor ace
And it happens a lot when English pop songs and novels are translated into Chinese

They “add” stuff, because Chinese is a lot more condensed than other languages, so they feel the need to add a bit of flair/flowery language/content that wasn’t there in the source text, to make it feel “less bland”
somehow gibberish turns into vow of death
Meanwhile when Chinese is translated into English it’s either a huge wall of text or the translator has to cut stuff
